title: requires(never) is a doc comment with extra syntax — will silently drift
description: requires(never) strips the check in all build modes and is described as 'documentation only'. A contract that is never checked will never be caught when it becomes wrong. If the function changes such that the precondition is violated, requires(never) provides no feedback. It is a doc comment that callers may mistake for an enforced invariant. The use case given (hot loops that are too expensive to check) is real, but the mechanism is unsafe. A better design would require explicit opt-in annotation in tests to verify requires(never) contracts offline.
